full_prompt: Design the open-frame chassis for a SAUVC (Singapore AUV Challenge) AUV compliant with SAUVC Rulebook v6. Vehicle bounding box 1500 mm x 800 mm x 500 mm. Pool-depth rated 5 m (SAUVC pool). Vehicle dry mass 35 kg. The frame is a bolted assembly of extruded aluminum 6063-T6 bars (25 mm x 25 mm square cross-section, 3 mm wall) forming a 3D open cage; the frame itself is the subject of design but shall be idealized and submitted as a single STEP assembly treated as one part. Material: 6063-T6 aluminum, E = 68.3 GPa, yield = 214 MPa, ultimate = 241 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. The frame carries: 6 thrusters (mounted at 6 faces of the frame; each applies nominal 50 N static thrust, peak 80 N); 1 pressure hull (8 kg dry); 1 battery pod (4 kg dry); acoustic array (2 kg). Total applied-in-frame mass 35 kg. Load cases: (R1) Handling: the full 35 kg vehicle lifted by a single top handle at mid-length => 35 x 9.81 = 343 N concentrated vertical load with 2x dynamic amplification = 687 N; max von Mises in any frame member <= yield / 2.0 = 107 MPa. (R2) Thruster combined: 6 thrusters at peak 80 N each applied in the worst-combined direction (e.g., 2 surge + 2 sway + 2 heave); max von Mises <= 107 MPa. (R3) Impact: 500 N concentrated load applied to any corner (pool wall strike); max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 143 MPa. (R4) Hydrostatic buoyancy: frame shall maintain its geometry within +-0.5 mm over its long axis at 5 m depth (50 kPa external); this is effectively a stiffness check - deflection under hydrostatic differential acting on any enclosed bladder-like members <= 0.5 mm (no closed-volume members in this design, so geometric by inspection). (R5) Frame structural mass <= 4 kg. (R6) Kill-switch accessibility: kill switch mount shall be reachable from any accessible face within 500 mm (geometric). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3). Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 frame assembly as single structural idealization, bolt schedule, and a report listing frame mass, max von Mises per case in MPa.
short_prompt:
Design the open-frame chassis for a SAUVC (Singapore AUV Challenge) AUV, compliant with SAUVC Rulebook v6. The frame is a bolted assembly of 25 x 25 mm square 6063-T6 extruded aluminum bars (3 mm wall) forming a 3D open cage; submit as a single STEP assembly idealized as one part. Vehicle dry mass 35 kg; pool depth 5 m. Frame carries 6 thrusters (one per face, 50 N static / 80 N peak each), a pressure hull (8 kg), a battery pod (4 kg), and an acoustic array (2 kg).

Envelope: bounding 1500 x 800 x 500 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 handling: 35 kg lifted by a single top handle at mid-length with 2 x dynamic amplification = 687 N vertical.
- LC2 thruster combined: 6 x 80 N peak thrust in the worst combined direction (2 surge + 2 sway + 2 heave).
- LC3 impact: 500 N concentrated at any corner (pool-wall strike).
- LC4 hydrostatic geometry: frame long-axis deflection under 5 m depth (50 kPa) <= 0.5 mm (geometric; no closed-volume members).

prompt.title: SAUVC AUV open-frame chassis
prompt.brief: 6063-T6 25 mm extrusion open cage, 35 kg vehicle, 5 m pool depth, handle-lift + thruster + corner-impact load cases.

boundary_conditions.mounting: handle fixed (for R1), lumped mass coupled at pressure-hull mount (for R2), free with thruster loads

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Not applicable (no closed pressure volumes in frame).
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Extrusion sections sized well above slender ratios.
